  loop correctly refusing to auto-merge a genuine two-sided divergence. Nothing's broken in
  the sync code itself — it's doing exactly what it should given the file contents. Happy to
  fix any/all of them if you want, but leaving as-is per your ask.

✻ Baked for 1m 13s

❯ wondering if we arrived at this state becasue of changes to murder codebase and these are
  old files, or is the current pipeline capable of leading to wedged state like this?

● Good question — let me distinguish "stale artifacts" from "reachable today." Let me check
  the history and whether the current writer could ever produce these.

● Bash(cd /home/user/Documents/code/murder; echo "=== schema.py PlanStatus history ==="; git
      log --oneline -L '/class PlanStatus/,/^class /:murder/work/plans/schema.py' 2>/…)
  ⎿  === schema.py PlanStatus history ===
     2e8fbf3 v1: initial scaffold + Collaborator chat wiring
     … +11 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(cd /home/user/Documents/code/murder
      echo "=== jk-crash file history ==="; git log --oneline --
      .murder/plans/plan-jk-crash.md | head…)
  ⎿  === jk-crash file history ===
     === tui-polish file history ===
     === done-crow-cleanup file history ===
     === is .murder tracked at all? ===

● .murder/plans/ is gitignored (runtime artifacts), so no per-file history. The schema answer
  is decisive though: PlanStatus has only ever had draft/accepted/superseded since the
  initial scaffold — open and active were never valid in this codebase. Let me confirm the
  writer can't emit them and look at the full frontmatter.

● Bash(cd /home/user/Documents/code/murder
      echo "=== parser parse/render/write ==="; sed -n '1,200p' murder/work/plans/parser.py)
  ⎿  === parser parse/render/write ===
     """Plan markdown parser/writer."""
     … +109 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

✶ Noodling… (26s · ↑ 1.5k tokens)
  ⎿  Tip: Say "fan out subagents" and Claude sends a team. Each one digs deep so nothing gets
     missed.
